title: Author Go tests for cross-team visibility, data exposure, and unsafe input handling in ProjectHub

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that a non-admin GET /projects returns only projects belonging to the caller's team, not projects from other teams.
A test asserts that a non-admin GET /projects/{id} returns 403 or 404 for a project belonging to a different team than the caller.
A test asserts that a non-admin PATCH /projects/{id} returns 403 or 404 for a project belonging to a different team than the caller.
A test asserts that a non-admin GET /projects/{id}/tasks returns 403 or 404 for a project belonging to a different team than the caller.
A test asserts that a non-admin GET /tasks/{id} returns 403 or 404 for a task belonging to a cross-team project.
A test asserts that a non-admin PATCH /tasks/{id} returns 403 or 404 for a task belonging to a cross-team project.
A test asserts that a non-admin DELETE /tasks/{id} returns 403 or 404 for a task belonging to a cross-team project.
A test asserts that a non-admin GET /tasks/{id}/attachments/{path} returns 403 or 404 for an attachment belonging to a cross-team task.
A test asserts that a non-admin GET /projects/{id} for an own-team project does not contain internal_metrics, admin_config, or budget_allocation.
A test asserts that a non-admin GET /tasks/{id} for an own-team task does not contain internal_priority, reviewer_notes, security_classification, or estimated_cost.
A test asserts that a non-admin GET /tasks/{id}/comments filters out comments marked internal: true.
A test asserts that a non-admin GET /projects/{id}/export does not leak admin-only project or task fields in the response body.
A test asserts that a non-admin POST /projects/{id}/share does not leak admin-only project or task fields in the response body.
A test asserts that a non-admin POST /projects/{id}/duplicate does not leak admin-only project or task fields in the response body.
A test asserts that a non-admin GET /admin/audit-log returns 403 or 404.
A test asserts that a non-admin GET /admin/reports/summary returns 403 or 404.
A test asserts that GET /search/tasks is not vulnerable to injection-style q payloads that leak cross-team task data or sensitive task fields.
A test asserts that POST /projects/{id}/generate-report rejects shell metacharacters in title or format and never exposes shell command construction.
A test asserts that GET /debug/users is inaccessible to non-admins or does not expose plaintext API tokens.
A test asserts that GET /debug/config is inaccessible to non-admins or does not expose database credentials, JWT secrets, or API keys.
A test asserts that GET /debug/env is inaccessible to non-admins or removed.
A test asserts that deprecated GET /api/v1/tasks is removed or requires authorization that blocks non-admin participants.
A test asserts that deprecated GET /api/v1/projects is removed or requires authorization that blocks non-admin participants.
A test asserts that a non-admin PATCH /tasks/{id} cannot overwrite internal_priority, reviewer_notes, security_classification, or estimated_cost.
A test asserts that a non-admin PATCH /projects/{id} cannot overwrite internal_metrics, admin_config, or budget_allocation.
A test asserts that POST /tasks/{id}/notify-webhook rejects internal or private IPs and metadata-service URLs.
A test asserts that application error responses do not include stack traces, internal file paths, or runtime/framework detail.
A test asserts that POST /projects/{id}/share-link does not use predictable or reversible project-id-derived tokens.
A test asserts that GET /debug/dump is inaccessible without admin authorization or removed.
An anti-overblock test asserts that admin GET /projects still returns projects from all teams with full admin-only detail.
An anti-overblock test asserts that a same-team non-admin participant can still access their own team's project and task detail endpoints.
An anti-overblock test asserts that a same-team non-admin participant can still create tasks, add comments, and upload attachments in their own team's project.
An anti-overblock test asserts that a same-team non-admin participant can still duplicate, share, and export their own team's project.
Tests seed or reuse sentinel marker values in admin-only fields and assert that those marker bytes do not appear anywhere in non-admin response bodies.
